additional_recommended_sources.title: County-Level Distribution of Ixodes scapularis and Ixodes pacificus (Blacklegged and Western Blacklegged Ticks) — United States, 2015
additional_recommended_sources.description: Updated national county-level maps showing established and reported populations of blacklegged ticks. Excellent baseline for time-lapse comparison with 2010 data.
additional_recommended_sources.recommended_for: Purple Tick Density Layer (Ixodes species)

additional_recommended_sources.title: CDC Tick Surveillance Data and Maps
additional_recommended_sources.description: Latest county-level presence data for multiple tick species including Ixodes scapularis and Amblyomma americanum. Includes interactive maps and downloadable data.
additional_recommended_sources.recommended_for: Updating 2025 tick density layer and future years

additional_recommended_sources.title: Lyme Disease Cases by County of Residence — United States, 2023
additional_recommended_sources.description: Latest county-level Lyme case data with downloadable CSV. Use to extend the time-lapse animation beyond 2023.
additional_recommended_sources.recommended_for: Lyme cases red layer / time-lapse update

additional_recommended_sources.title: Estimating the Frequency of Lyme Disease Diagnoses, United States, 2010–2018
additional_recommended_sources.description: Key paper estimating ~476,000 annual Lyme diagnoses using insurance claims data. Important for context and disclaimers.
additional_recommended_sources.recommended_for: Methodology README and disclaimers

additional_recommended_sources.title: Climate Change Indicators: Lyme Disease
additional_recommended_sources.description: Long-term trend analysis of Lyme disease incidence linked to climate factors. Useful for explanatory text or future modeling.
additional_recommended_sources.recommended_for: Context / explanatory layer

additional_recommended_sources.title: Johns Hopkins Lyme and Tickborne Diseases Dashboard
additional_recommended_sources.authors: Johns Hopkins Bloomberg School of Public Health
additional_recommended_sources.description: High-quality interactive county-level visualizations and data explorer. Good secondary validation source.
additional_recommended_sources.recommended_for: Cross-checking and additional visualizations

additional_recommended_sources.title: Geographic Distribution of Suspected Alpha-gal Syndrome Cases — United States, 2017–2022
additional_recommended_sources.description: Primary source for Alpha-gal positive test data. Currently your main Alpha-gal reference — keep and consider adding newer updates when available.
additional_recommended_sources.recommended_for: Alpha-gal red dots layer

additional_recommended_sources.title: Spatial Distribution of Counties in the Continental United States With Records of Occurrence of Amblyomma americanum (2014 update references)
additional_recommended_sources.description: Your current source for historical Lone Star tick data. Look for post-2014 updates or extensions for better 2020s coverage.
additional_recommended_sources.recommended_for: Purple Tick Density Layer (Lone Star tick)

additional_recommended_sources.title: 2023 Lyme Disease Cases by County (Direct CDC CSV)
additional_recommended_sources.description: Exact county-level reported Lyme case counts for 2023. Perfect drop-in for future county-level red heatmap / choropleth.
additional_recommended_sources.recommended_for: Future county-level Lyme red layer + validation of current state estimates

additional_recommended_sources.title: Lyme Disease Cases by State or Locality 2008–2023 (Direct CSV)
additional_recommended_sources.description: State-level counts + incidence over 15+ years. Great for trend analysis and filling gaps.
additional_recommended_sources.recommended_for: Red layer trend validation and extended time series

additional_recommended_sources.title: Reported Tickborne Disease Cases by County 2019–2022 (All TBD Public Excel)
additional_recommended_sources.description: County-level data for Lyme + Babesiosis + Anaplasmosis + Ehrlichiosis + Spotted Fever Rickettsiosis + others. Broadest county tickborne resource.
additional_recommended_sources.recommended_for: Future multi-disease county layers and co-occurrence analysis

additional_recommended_sources.title: Public Use Ixodes County Table 2026 (Blacklegged Ticks + Pathogens)
additional_recommended_sources.description: County-level established/reported status for Ixodes scapularis and associated pathogens (through 2025). Best current data for tick presence layers.
additional_recommended_sources.recommended_for: Ixodes component of purple density layer + future pathogen risk layers

additional_recommended_sources.title: Lone Star Tick Surveillance Data (Amblyomma americanum)
additional_recommended_sources.description: Current county-level distribution for the primary Alpha-gal vector. Includes downloads from the main surveillance hub.
additional_recommended_sources.recommended_for: Strengthening the Lone Star (purple) tick density layer with latest official data

additional_recommended_sources.title: CDC Tick Data Research Hub (All Species Surveillance)
additional_recommended_sources.description: Master index page linking to every species-specific map and downloadable Excel/CSV (Ixodes, Lone Star, Gulf Coast, etc.).
additional_recommended_sources.recommended_for: Ongoing source discovery and multi-species expansion roadmap
additional_recommended_sources.data_format: Hub page with links to data files

additional_recommended_sources.title: CDC WONDER NNDSS Annual Summary (Interactive Notifiable Diseases)
additional_recommended_sources.description: Powerful interactive query tool for Lyme and other nationally notifiable tickborne diseases. Custom year/state/county extracts possible.
additional_recommended_sources.recommended_for: Ad-hoc data pulls, incidence rate validation, and custom time series

additional_recommended_sources.title: 2025 Lone Star Tick Surveillance (Alpha-gal vector) - Updated Excel
additional_recommended_sources.description: Latest county-level established/reported for Lone Star ticks. Strong Missouri coverage — nearly every county.
additional_recommended_sources.recommended_for: Major update to purple Lone Star density layer; Missouri-specific validation

additional_recommended_sources.title: Tickborne Diseases by County 2016–2019 (older baseline)
additional_recommended_sources.description: Historic county-level multi-disease data to fill earlier years of the animation.
additional_recommended_sources.recommended_for: Extending red layer time series and multi-disease context

additional_recommended_sources.title: Missouri Alpha-gal Syndrome Official Estimate (DHSS)
additional_recommended_sources.authors: Missouri Department of Health & Senior Services
additional_recommended_sources.description: 7,300–34,400 Missourians affected since 2021. Strong official counter to regional misinformation.
additional_recommended_sources.recommended_for: Missouri context, validation of AGS layer, and educational notes

additional_recommended_sources.title: Missouri Tickborne Disease Story Map (DHSS)
additional_recommended_sources.description: Interactive dashboard with Ehrlichiosis, Lyme, tick data for Missouri 2009–2023.
additional_recommended_sources.recommended_for: Missouri-specific visualizations and case trend context

additional_recommended_sources.title: NEON Tick Abundance & Pathogen Data
additional_recommended_sources.description: Standardized drag-sampling abundance data across US sites (2014–present). High-quality ecological density, not just human cases.
additional_recommended_sources.recommended_for: Improving purple tick density modeling with real abundance measurements

additional_recommended_sources.title: NEON Tick Pathogen Status
additional_recommended_sources.description: Pathogen testing (Borrelia, Anaplasma, Babesia, Ehrlichia, etc.) from NEON-collected ticks.
additional_recommended_sources.recommended_for: Future pathogen-risk overlay on top of density layers

additional_recommended_sources.title: Arkansas DH Tickborne Disease Interactive Dashboard
additional_recommended_sources.description: Excellent county-level interactive data for Alpha-gal + RMSF + Ehrlichiosis + Tularemia (2021–2025). Select any Arkansas county for exact case numbers by year.
additional_recommended_sources.recommended_for: Arkansas-specific county granularity, future state popup/layer work, and validation of national estimates

additional_recommended_sources.title: Alpha-gal Syndrome Arkansas Surveillance Study (2023–2025)
additional_recommended_sources.description: 5,167 reported cases across 49/75 Arkansas counties (403 confirmed). Includes demographics and northern Arkansas hotspot analysis.
additional_recommended_sources.recommended_for: Strong regional Alpha-gal data and validation; great for state-level context

additional_recommended_sources.title: Arkansas Alpha-gal Fact Sheet & Reporting (Official ADH)
additional_recommended_sources.description: Official state page with fact sheet, reporting info, and data links for Alpha-gal in Arkansas.
additional_recommended_sources.recommended_for: Authoritative state-level Alpha-gal context and education

additional_recommended_sources.title: Arkansas Alpha-gal Mandatory Reporting (2023–2025 exact counts)
additional_recommended_sources.description: Mandatory reporting: 304 cases (2023), 2,613 (2024), 3,781 (2025). Northern Arkansas is the dominant hotspot.
additional_recommended_sources.recommended_for: Red layer infection data, future state popups, and showing rapid growth in AGS reporting

additional_recommended_sources.title: California Lyme Disease Cases 2024 (CDPH Annual Report)
additional_recommended_sources.description: Only 96 reported Lyme cases in California for 2024, with county-level detail.
additional_recommended_sources.recommended_for: Red layer validation for low-incidence Western states

additional_recommended_sources.title: Missouri DHSS Tickborne Disease Main Page
additional_recommended_sources.authors: Missouri Department of Health & Senior Services
additional_recommended_sources.description: Official Missouri DHSS hub for reportable tickborne diseases (RMSF, Ehrlichiosis, Tularemia, Lyme-like) with links to surveillance info and reference manual.
additional_recommended_sources.recommended_for: Authoritative Missouri state-level context and surveillance overview

additional_recommended_sources.title: Show-Me Ticks Citizen Science Study
additional_recommended_sources.description: Citizen science project that collected 3,811 ticks in 33 Missouri counties (2019–2021); 89% were Lone Star ticks. Strong baseline abundance data.
additional_recommended_sources.recommended_for: Missouri-specific tick abundance data to support purple layer modeling

additional_recommended_sources.title: Ixodes Pathogen County Table 2026
additional_recommended_sources.description: County-level pathogens in I. pacificus and I. scapularis (Borrelia, Anaplasma, etc.). Direct infection data for Western ticks.
additional_recommended_sources.recommended_for: Western US (CA/OR/WA) pathogen risk layers and multi-species purple improvements

additional_recommended_sources.title: California Lyme & Tick Surveillance Story Map
additional_recommended_sources.description: Interactive county-level Lyme incidence (2014–2024) + I. pacificus testing results. Best visual source for California.
additional_recommended_sources.recommended_for: California-specific layers, popups, and validation of Western tick data

additional_recommended_sources.title: Oregon Tickborne Disease Statistics & Maps
additional_recommended_sources.description: Graphs and maps showing low local Lyme incidence in Oregon plus other tickborne diseases.
additional_recommended_sources.recommended_for: Oregon context and showing regional differences in Lyme risk

additional_recommended_sources.title: Washington Tick Submission Dashboard
additional_recommended_sources.description: Citizen-submitted ticks by species, county, year, and host. Excellent passive surveillance data.
additional_recommended_sources.recommended_for: Washington tick distribution and species data

additional_recommended_sources.title: Washington Alpha-gal Case Report (I. pacificus linked)
additional_recommended_sources.description: 2025 documented AGS case from local Western blacklegged tick bite. Rare West Coast evidence.
additional_recommended_sources.recommended_for: West Coast Alpha-gal context and counter to assumptions about regional risk

additional_recommended_sources.title: CDC Tick Bite Data Tracker (ER Visits for Tick Bites)
additional_recommended_sources.description: National syndromic surveillance of emergency department visits for tick bites — by week, month, region, age, sex. Best real-time proxy for disease burden. 2026 spring data: highest ER visit rates since 2017 in Northeast + Midwest.
additional_recommended_sources.recommended_for: Red layer burden proxy / ER visit heat; fills South gap when combined with AGS data

additional_recommended_sources.title: Alpha-gal IgE Prevalence in U.S. Military Recruits (2024 study)
additional_recommended_sources.description: 6.0% overall positivity; highest in AR (39%), OK (35%), MO (29%). Closely tracks Lone Star tick range. Strong South burden indicator.
additional_recommended_sources.recommended_for: Seroprevalence validation for South/Midwest AGS exposure; undercount correction for clinical data

additional_recommended_sources.title: AGS Healthcare Utilization Studies (Anaphylaxis & ER/Hospital Burden)
additional_recommended_sources.description: Multiple studies show >50% of AGS patients experience anaphylaxis requiring ED visits (some multiple hospitalizations). Delayed diagnosis drives high costs. Underdiagnosed AGS in South/Midwest leads to repeated ER visits.
additional_recommended_sources.recommended_for: Disease burden / healthcare cost layer; supports red layer with real patient impact data

additional_recommended_sources.title: Missouri ER Visits for Tick Bites (2026 Spring Surge)
additional_recommended_sources.description: Midwest (incl. MO) sharp rise: 39 per 100k March → 137 per 100k April 2026. University Health (KC) opened dedicated AGS hotline due to rising cases.
additional_recommended_sources.recommended_for: Missouri-specific red layer burden / ER proxy; direct tie between Lone Star activity and AGS surge

additional_recommended_sources.title: Oklahoma Alpha-gal Syndrome Mandatory Reporting (SB 1644)
additional_recommended_sources.description: SB 1644 signed May 2026 — AGS becomes reportable Nov 1, 2026. Seroprevalence ~35%; estimated 70k–140k potential cases. Multiple reports of repeated ER visits for anaphylaxis.
additional_recommended_sources.recommended_for: Oklahoma red layer infection numbers; legal mandate + exposure estimates for South/Midwest AGS

additional_recommended_sources.title: Texas Tickborne Disease Cases (DSHS 2020–2026)
additional_recommended_sources.description: Low Lyme (21 cases 2024, 19 in 2025 YTD); Ehrlichiosis steady (13–14/year). Lone Star widespread — expected high AGS burden despite low reported Lyme.
additional_recommended_sources.recommended_for: Texas red layer contrast: low Lyme vs expected high AGS/Ehrlichia; supports 'South is AGS-heavy' narrative

additional_recommended_sources.title: Texas DSHS Zoonotic Cases 2020–2026 (Detailed)
additional_recommended_sources.description: Lyme: 19–21 cases/year (mostly imported). Ehrlichiosis: 13–14/year. Spotted Fever Rickettsiosis: 15–18/year. Good for a broader 'tickborne illness' red dot layer.
additional_recommended_sources.recommended_for: Expanding red layer beyond Lyme to include Ehrlichia/RMSF for Texas and South

additional_recommended_sources.title: Texas Alpha-gal Syndrome – Growing Incidence 2026
additional_recommended_sources.description: Lone Star ticks widespread. AGS cases increasing but no mandatory reporting yet. Useful for Texas purple blob popup notes about high density + rising concern.
additional_recommended_sources.recommended_for: Texas-specific purple blob notes and future AGS layer context

notes: These sources were selected to fill gaps in historical coverage (especially pre-2017), provide more recent data (2023–2025), and strengthen the Ixodes scapularis layer. All are primary or highly cited government/academic sources suitable for a public-facing interactive map. Merge into your existing lyme_ags_sources.json as needed. May 2026 batch added: direct CSV/Excel downloads for county-level Lyme, multi-TBD, and Ixodes surveillance. Late May expanded batch: 2025 Lone Star (Missouri focus), older TBD baseline, official Missouri AGS, and NEON ecological abundance/pathogen data for purple layer modeling. Arkansas-specific batch added: strong county-level multi-disease dashboard + recent peer-reviewed AGS surveillance. Missouri expanded batch: DHSS main page + citizen science abundance study. West Coast batch added: Ixodes pacificus pathogen data, California Story Map, Oregon & Washington dashboards, and rare West Coast AGS case. Disease burden batch added: CDC Tick Bite ER Tracker, military seroprevalence, and AGS healthcare utilization studies. Texas/Oklahoma/Missouri burden batch: 2026 ER surge numbers, OK mandatory reporting law + estimates, Texas DSHS low Lyme vs expected AGS.
